scenario: Delta Retail deploys a new version of an order process in Camunda 8.8 with 8000 orders per day already running on the previous version. The team must move every running instance from version 3 to version 4 without restarting them and with explicit mappings for activities that changed identifiers.
question: Which Operate feature moves running instances between two versions of the same process definition with explicit activity mappings?

options.text: Process instance migration in Operate moves running instances to the target version and accepts explicit source-to-target activity mappings
Process instance modification in Operate moves running instances to the target version when the operator manually edits each running instance
Process redeployment in Operate moves running instances to the target version automatically using the latest deployment of the definition
Process instance restart in Operate moves running instances to the target version by cancelling them and starting fresh instances on the new version

a.text: Correct. Operate's process instance migration moves running instances between two versions and accepts an explicit mapping of source to target activities.

b.text: Incorrect. Modification edits tokens within the same instance's current definition; it does not move instances to a different version.

c.text: Incorrect. Redeployment creates a new version but does not automatically move existing running instances to that version.

d.text: Incorrect. Restart drops in-flight state; migration is the documented mechanism that preserves running instance progress while changing the version.

explanation: Operate's process instance migration moves running instances between two versions of a definition with explicit activity mappings, preserving in-flight work.
